pages.full_text:
[...shop] Simon know has closed. There are lots of elderflower trees on the incredibly straight A1 going north. We've reached Beverley by 3, but parking is difficult so we're going straight to the campsite. Willow Lane Campsite, 3 minutes from town, Didyoga.

[photo of Beverley Minster]

__Beverley Minster__
West Front + South Side.
A Saxon church was here about 700. St. John from Lindisfarne did miracles. Mediaeval parts well-attuned to each other in the present Minster. Sanctuary

rights. Beautiful & interesting exhibits - [strikethrough] Sanctuary
[illegible]
photo_captions: Beverley Minster West Front + South Side. A Saxon church was here about 700. St. John from Lindisfarne did miracles. Mediaeval parts well-attuned to each other in the present Minster. Sanctuary
pages.notes: Top-left corner of page is cropped/not visible so handwritten page number cannot be confirmed. Text at top-left is cut off at left edge of scan — 'shop Simon know has closed' is the visible fragment of a longer sentence. 'Didyoga' at end of first paragraph may be a place name or abbreviation — uncertain reading. Bottom text after photo is partially obscured; a word appears struck through before 'Sanctuary'. The A1 reference suggests travel northward through England.

pages.full_text:
The campsite is an 11 minute walk from [4?] Town. There's [are] incredible building projects for housing near the site. Until WW2 there could have been fields up to the Minster. V. nice paths & cycle ways. We had coffee & shopped & enjoyed the Minster, then returned for Tinder & had fish & chips in town for lunch. Reading in the van. 3 other vans/caravans on site, & a man who spent hours doing exercise!

There are lots of Georgian houses in Beverley. The campsite has massive house-building next to it.

__Thursday__
Eve walked Tinder & we set off for Bowes for lunch with the Fishers. We had a super day, chatting & admired their garden & an hour's walk. We got back for a cup of tea just before torrential rain. Both looking well.
Now we are at Pecknell Farm campsite on the [dale] way to the upper Tees Valley.
Amazing, well-built dry-stone wall up drive Ash dale-[?Pack]. Swallows.

__Friday__
We stopped for bread on the way & drove on to the Killhope (!) lead mine. It was free because
pages.notes: Top-right corner handwritten page number: appears to show a number but is partially cut off — possibly '49' or similar; recorded as null. 'Tinder' appears to be a dog's name used throughout. 'the Fishers' are presumably friends/family visited at Bowes. 'Pecknell Farm campsite' — could also be 'Pecknell' or similar; best reading given. 'Ash dale-[?Pack]' — second element of this place name is uncertain. The word 'dale' appears written above the line as a correction/insertion near 'way to the upper Tees Valley'. Page text ends mid-sentence ('It was free because') — continues on next spread. Year cannot be determined from content visible on this page; no year stated. Volume B covers 2003–2019.
